commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Myself. "Have I not placed my trust in him? "God alone. (Haydock)
All my other friends have abandoned me, ver. 15. (Calmet)
Can they wonder if I express my grief? (Haydock)
Familiar. Hebrew, "is wisdom removed far from me? "(Haydock)
Has my strength abandoned me, so that I cannot be recognized? (Calmet)

commentaries.author: Gregory The Dialogist
commentaries.content: 27. It is now made clear to whom the mind of the stricken man had recourse for hope, seeing that he declares that there was no hope to him in himself; but because he intimates that in himself he was weak, for the earning [or ‘to (shew) the merit of.’] of yet greater strength, let him add how he was even forsaken by his neighbours, My friends also departed from me [V. thus]. But mark, he that was despised without, is seated within upon the throne of judgment. For at the moment that he declares himself forsaken.

methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
